comment: Please avoid the female staff member who takes orders here. I was clearly standing at the front waiting to order, but she deliberately tried to take the order from people behind me first. When I placed my order, I told her my name was Michael, but she wrote it down as “Michelle.” Because of that, my order never came out and I ended up waiting all day. When I checked, I found that the order had been recorded under “Michelle.” This is clearly discriminatory and unacceptable behavior.

I also ordered coffee and a sandwich together, but the coffee came out much earlier while the sandwich took quite a long time. That can happen, but the attitude and lack of friendliness made the experience unpleasant. I hope customer service improves, as I used to really like this place.

comment: So disappointing! Ordered a HCT ham cheese tomato right ? No it’s mortadella (cheap cuts of ham), tiny bit of cheese and tomato relish no real tomato, $17.50 for a fake toastie how does one get this wrong !!!

Don’t come here not worth the hype or what you hear

Honestly one of the most disappointing food experiences I’ve had in a long time. I ordered the chicken and it was absolutely terrible. It was way too fatty and just dripping with oil. Every bite felt like it had been soaked in grease rather than properly cooked.

The flavor wasn’t any better either. It honestly tasted like someone crushed up a packet of salt and vinegar chips and dumped it on wet chicken. Just overpowering, weird, and completely unpleasant.

Then there was the almond croissant. That thing was a disaster. The top was rock hard like it had been sitting out all day, and the inside was cold. Not even slightly warm just cold and stale.

For a place that gets so much hype, this was a massive letdown. Definitely not worth the money or the reputation people give it.

comment: Was yum, fast coffee and fresh sandwiches.
Underrated donuts I would say. Got the strawberry, maple glaze and cinnamon and every donut is on point. Would definitely return.

I was surprised to see a Hector’s Deli kiosk pop up on Bourke Street right outside the H&M store this week. I absolutely love their other locations in Little Collins, South Melbourne and the bigger one in Richmond with all the pastries, so I had to stop when I spotted it.

Even though it was only 10am I could not resist grabbing a maple glazed donut and it did not disappoint. So good.

This kiosk seems to focus just on pastries rather than the sandwiches they are known for, which makes it more of a quick grab and go stop. The pastries looked great and everything felt very on brand for Hector’s.

My only suggestion would be to offer some kind of coffee option as well. Even something simple like canned iced lattes would be perfect because I definitely would have grabbed one with the donut.

Still a great little addition to the CBD and a dangerous discovery for anyone who already loves Hector’s like I do.
Best donuts in Melbourne
Love it

Donuts are not too sweet so they are dangerously good!

The strawberry milkshake and the maple glaze ones are my favourites.
